phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q bombs out-range nearly every mid — chunk his last-hit attempts from angles the wave can't block.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 your minefield satchel poke beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Your minefield satchel poke out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control at level 3 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your minefield satchel poke beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your minefield satchel poke on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your minefield satchel poke in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Anivia's wall-frost poke control own this window — your minefield satchel poke don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q bombs out-range nearly every mid — chunk his last-hit attempts from angles the wave can't block.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W Satchel online — your disengage button. You can now take a greedy trade and blast yourself out of the answer.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: E minefield behind you makes you nearly unchaseable. Bounce Q off the wave so he can't read the angle.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your minefield satchel poke out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — minefield satchel poke edge out the stardust ramp control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control edge out your minefield satchel poke on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Shadowflame + Torch makes the full rotation lethal, but the map matters more — rotate R to sieges and skirmishes.

phases.why: Q bombs out-range nearly every mid — chunk his last-hit attempts from angles the wave can't block.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W Satchel online — your disengage button. You can now take a greedy trade and blast yourself out of the answer.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: E minefield behind you makes you nearly unchaseable. Bounce Q off the wave so he can't read the angle.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Stack Manaflow and chip him below 60% — that's the zone where R + jungle help executes. Track his engage before every step-up.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Mega Inferno Bomb online — any chipped target or crashing wave is now your gold. Hold it for the sub-60% HP window.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Blackfire Torch spike — your Q chunks become unhealable burn chip. He now chooses between farm and HP every wave.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Shadowflame + Torch makes the full rotation lethal, but the map matters more — rotate R to sieges and skirmishes.
